name: Filter bank
description: This algorithm is based on the disting EX algorithm of the same name. The disting EX manual states “this algorithm provides a bank of eight parallel stereo bandpass filters or resonators. The filters' levels can be controlled manually, via CV, or with envelopes driven from gates, and their pitch can also be set manually or via CV or MIDI. The resonators in particular are good for being played as chords over MIDI, in the manner of the Alesis Quadraverb Plus”. This implementation goes further in that the number of filters can be increased to 12, and the filters can be mono, stereo, or up to 8 channels wide.
gui_description: The display shows the notes to which each filter is tuned, overlaid on a bar to represent the envelope level of the voice.

specifications.name: Filters
specifications.description: The number of filters in the bank.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The number of audio input busses (e.g. set to 2 for stereo).

parameters.name: Resonance/Q
parameters.description: Sets the filter gain (for resonators) or resonance (for bandpass/multiband).

parameters.name: Attack time
parameters.description: The envelope attack time. The scale is exponential, from 1ms up to 4s.

parameters.name: Release time
parameters.description: The envelope release time. The scale is exponential, from 1ms up to 4s.

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: An overall gain control.

parameters.name: Pitch
parameters.description: The filter frequency/pitch, as a MIDI note number.

parameters.name: Gate
parameters.description: The filter gate.

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: The filter’s gain.

parameters.name: MIDI channel
parameters.description: The MIDI channel to listen on.

parameters.name: MPE channels
parameters.description: Controls how the algorithm will respond to MPE.

parameters.name: I2C channel
parameters.description: Sets the I2C channel.

parameters.name: Controlled voices
parameters.description: Sets the number of filters that will be controlled by MIDI or CV/gate. By reducing this, you can elect to control some filters by MIDI while the rest remain under manual control.

parameters.name: Transpose
parameters.description: Coarse tuning control.

parameters.name: Fine tune
parameters.description: Fine tuning control.

parameters.name: Sustain
parameters.description: Directly controls the sustain (like a MIDI sustain pedal).

parameters.name: Bend range
parameters.description: The MIDI pitch bend range.

parameters.name: Unison
parameters.description: The number of voices to play simultaneously for each note triggered.

parameters.name: Unison detune
parameters.description: The detune amount when Unison is active.

parameters.name: Sustain mode
parameters.description: The standard polysynth sustain mode parameter.

parameters.name: Press -> volume
parameters.description: The amount by which pressure (per note for MPE) affects the note volume.

parameters.name: MPE Y -> volume
parameters.description: The amount by which the MPE Y dimension affects the note volume.
